message:
fix(normalize,packs,history): close attached-redirection bypass in command scanning

Quoted command words with shell redirections attached directly to the executable
name could slip past the quick-reject keyword gate, which meant dangerous git
commands such as `"git">/dev/null reset --hard` were normalized too late to be
sent through the destructive-pack evaluation path. This commit closes that hole
and updates the history tests to assert against the current FTS behavior.

- split attached redirection operators away from normalized command words before
  downstream keyword matching, while explicitly leaving numeric file-descriptor
  redirects like `2>/dev/null` untouched
- add regression coverage for quoted command words with attached redirects and
  for the pack quick-reject fast path so the bypass cannot reappear silently
- keep the variable-assignment/data fast path intact by falling back to a full
  normalized keyword scan only for narrow shell-syntax rewrites involving `<` or `>`
- update the history integration tests to query FTS row ids directly instead of
  relying on count semantics that drifted under the underlying sqlite layer

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
